title: Stock Futures Higher, Oil Elevated as European and Asian Markets Shut
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Stock futures were higher, oil prices elevated, and U.S. Treasurys steady amid ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. European markets were closed, and Asian
excerpt: Stock futures were higher, oil prices elevated, and U.S. Treasurys steady amid ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. European markets were closed, and Asian markets showed mixed reactions. Brent crude oil remained above $100 per barrel.

content.introduction: Stock futures were higher, oil prices elevated, and U.S. Treasurys steady amid ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. European markets were closed, and Asian markets showed mixed reactions. Brent crude oil remained above $100 per barrel.
body: WSJ Markets (@WSJmarkets). 4 likes. Stock Futures Higher, Oil Elevated as European and Asian Markets Shut.

# European stock markets fall and oil and gas prices jump as strait of Hormuz ‘chaos’ worries investors – as it happened. * Tehran has ‘no plans to participate’ in new talks, state media reports, as it accuses US of violating ceasefire. **European stock markets have dropped at the start of trading, as last Friday’s optimism about a Middle East peace deal evaporates.**. In London, the **FTSE** **100** has dropped by 42 points, or 0.4%, to 10,626 points, away from a six-week high at the end of last week. The “chaos” over the strait of Hormuz has left the markets on edge, reports **Chris Beauchamp,** chief market analyst at investing and trading platform **IG:**. While Iran has declared it closed, markets seem to be, as ever during this crisis, looking on the bright side. > US futures are down, and Europe is expected to open lower, but most of the gains are still intact.
